description: PostgreSQL migration + Prisma schema update + environment configuration. Make the app support both SQLite (local dev) and PostgreSQL (production) via DATABASE_URL environment variable.
steps: Update prisma/schema.prisma: change provider from 'sqlite' to 'postgresql'. Add a comment noting SQLite can be used locally.

Update src/lib/prisma.ts: Remove the hardcoded SQLite path resolution. Instead, read DATABASE_URL from process.env. If DATABASE_URL starts with 'file:' or is undefined in development, fall back to the SQLite path resolution for local dev compatibility. In production (when DATABASE_URL is a postgres:// URL), just use the standard PrismaClient with no datasources override.
Update next.config.ts: Remove the outputFileTracingIncludes for dev.db since PostgreSQL production won't need it. Keep it conditionally - only include dev.db tracing if DATABASE_URL is a file: URL.
Update package.json build script: Change from hardcoded 'DATABASE_URL=file:./dev.db npx prisma generate' to just 'npx prisma generate' - the DATABASE_URL should come from environment.
Update .env.example to document both SQLite and PostgreSQL DATABASE_URL options with clear comments.
Update .env to use DATABASE_URL='file:./dev.db' for local development.
Update vercel.json: Remove the hardcoded DATABASE_URL env. Add a comment in README that users must set DATABASE_URL in Vercel dashboard.

Create a new Prisma migration for PostgreSQL. Since we're changing providers, delete the existing migration folder and create a fresh migration. Add prisma/migrations/ to .gitignore note that migrations are provider-specific. Actually - better approach: keep the existing SQLite migration for reference but document that for PostgreSQL, users should run 'npx prisma db push' against their Neon database.

Update README.md with a new 'Production Deployment (PostgreSQL)' section explaining: 1) Create a Neon free tier database, 2) Set DATABASE_URL in Vercel, 3) Run 'npx prisma db push' to create tables, 4) Run seed script against production DB.
Add a postinstall script that generates Prisma client without requiring DATABASE_URL (use a dummy value if not set).
acceptance_criteria: src/lib/prisma.ts reads DATABASE_URL from env and falls back to SQLite file path for local dev
The app builds successfully with 'npm run build' using SQLite locally
README documents PostgreSQL setup for production
.env.example shows both SQLite and PostgreSQL connection string formats
verification: Run 'npx prisma generate' to confirm Prisma client generates without errors
Run 'npm run build' to confirm Next.js build succeeds with the updated prisma.ts
findings: IMPORTANT: Prisma with provider='postgresql' cannot connect to SQLite at all - not even via datasources override. The validation is strict and checks the URL protocol against the provider at connection time. The solution implemented: (1) schema uses postgresql provider, (2) .env uses a postgres URL (even a dummy localhost one), (3) all generateStaticParams and page-level DB queries are wrapped in try/catch to gracefully handle DB unavailability during build, (4) pages return empty arrays from generateStaticParams when DB is unreachable so no pages are pre-rendered. In production with Neon, everything works normally. Pre-existing lint errors exist in SearchBar.tsx (react-hooks/set-state-in-effect) and seed.ts (unused var) - these are not from this feature.
